title: Abstract P502: Blood Pressure Variability and Neurodegenerative Biomarkers: The Multidomain Alzheimer Preventive Trial
abstract:
Background:
 Increased Blood Pressure (BP) Variability (BPV) may represent an alteration in physiological homeostatic patterns, typical of late life, and has been associated with cerebrovascular disease, cognitive impairment and dementia, including Alzheimer’s disease. Accumulation of Brain beta amyloid (Aβ) and neurofilament light chain (NFL) release are typical biomarkers of neurodegeneration, whose assessment requires neuroimaging and cerebrospinal fluid (CFS) measurements, thus limiting their use in large-scale screening of the aging populations. Nevertheless, plasma Aβ42/40 ratio is associated with CSF Aβ42/40 ratio, similarly plasma NFL levels correlate with NFL in CFS. Furthermore, blood progranulin (PGL) is also considered a neurodegenerative biomarker, since deficiency exerts pleiotropic effects on the development, maintenance, stress response, and ageing of neural circuit.
 
 
 Objective:
 We aimed to explore the association of visit-to-visit BPV with blood concentrations of neurodegenerative biomarkers in a population of older adults.
 
 
 Methods:
 Participants were selected among community-dwelling participants aged ≥70 years from the Multidomain Alzheimer Preventive Trial (MAPT) Study. Plasma blood sample was collected 12 months after enrolment and BP was assessed every six months from enrollment (0, 6, 12 months). Systolic BPV (SBPV) and diastolic BPV (DBPV) were determined through several indicators considering BP change over time, the order of measurements and formulas independent of mean BP levels. Multivariable linear regressions were used to assess the association between visit-to-visit BPV and either PGL, Aβ42/40 and NFL.
 
 
 Results:
 The study population consisted of 470 participants (mean age 75.7 ± 4.5 years; female, 59.7%). Visit-to-visit BPV (all indicators, both systolic and diastolic) was not associated with blood levels of neurodegenerative biomarkers after adjustment for demographics, BMI, MAPT randomization group, education, baseline BP, antihypertensive drugs, diabetes mellitus, ischemic heart disease, atrial fibrillation, stroke: per 1-SD increase in systolic BPV (coefficient of variation %): Aβ42/40: adjusted β (SE) = -0.07 (0.04), p=0.13 and NFL: adjusted β (SE) = -0.06 (0.04), p=0.19.
 
 
 Conclusion:
 Neither systolic nor diastolic visit-to-visit BPV were associated with neurodegenerative blood biomarkers (Aβ42/40, NFL, and PGL) in a population of older adults. Further studies with longer-term assessment of BPV are needed.
